summary: Joel shares a brief, humorous anecdote about starting a Windows CE development project. He notes that for four years he was annoyed by Windows CE documentation cluttering up his MSDN searches. After finally figuring out how to remove the WinCE docs from MSDN, he ironically finds himself needing them for a new project.
key_points: MSDN's documentation search was cluttered by Windows CE content that was irrelevant to most developers
The irony of removing unwanted documentation only to immediately need it illustrates Murphy's Law in software development
Developer tooling and documentation organization was a persistent pain point in the early 2000s Microsoft ecosystem
money_quotes: For the last 4 years I've been furious every time I try to find something in MSDN, and the bloody WinCE documentation kept getting in the way.
After I finally figured out how to delete the WinCE stuff from MSDN, of course, I find myself starting a WinCE development project. Blah.
key_insight: Developer tools have a way of punishing you with ironic timing — the moment you clean up your environment is the moment you need what you removed.
